title: Fast Food Without Leaving Your Car!
story: Cars line up at drive-thru windows across America every July 24 for National Drive-Thru Day! This fun holiday celebrates the convenience of getting food, coffee, and even medicine without stepping out of your vehicle. People share photos of their favorite drive-thru meals on social media, and some restaurants offer special deals or freebies. From burgers to ice cream, drive-thrus make life easier for busy families and anyone who wants a quick bite on the go.
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Drive-Thru Day?
activity_choices: Photos of their favorite drive-thru meals
Videos of them walking into restaurants
activity_answer: Photos of their favorite drive-thru meals
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest thing you think you could buy at a drive-thru?

title: Cousins Day: Family Fun Beyond Siblings
story: Every July 24, families across the country celebrate Cousins Day by reaching out to those special relatives who aren't quite siblings but feel just as close! Cousins text funny memories, share old photos on social media, and plan family gatherings or video calls. Some families organize cousin reunions with games, barbecues, and storytelling sessions where older relatives share embarrassing childhood stories. Many people post throwback pictures with hashtags like #CousinsDay and #FamilyLove. It's the perfect excuse to reconnect with those family members who knew all your secrets growing up but somehow still liked you anyway!
activity_question: What do many people post on social media for Cousins Day?
activity_choices: Photos of their pets dressed as cousins
Pictures of their favorite pizza toppings
follow_up_question: What's your favorite memory with a cousin or close family friend?

title: Tell an Old Joke Day: Vintage Laughs
story: Every July 24, people dust off their corniest, oldest jokes and share them with everyone they meet! Tell an Old Joke Day celebrates those classic jokes that have been making people groan and giggle for decades. Kids tell knock-knock jokes at school, parents share puns at dinner, and grandparents finally get to use all those silly riddles they've been saving up. Social media fills with vintage one-liners and dad jokes that are so old they're actually funny again. The best part? The older and cheesier the joke, the better! It's a day when being corny is totally cool.
activity_question: What makes a joke perfect for Tell an Old Joke Day?
activity_choices: It has to be brand new
The older and cheesier, the better
activity_answer: The older and cheesier, the better
follow_up_question: What's the oldest joke you know that still makes you laugh?

title: Flying High on Amelia Earhart Day
story: Every July 24, people celebrate National Amelia Earhart Day to honor one of the most famous pilots in history! Amelia was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean, proving that girls could do anything boys could do. On this special day, aviation museums host special exhibits about her incredible flights. Schools teach kids about her brave adventures in the sky. Many people share her inspiring quotes on social media, like "Adventure is worthwhile in itself." Some families even visit airports or take flying lessons to follow in her footsteps. It's a perfect day to dream big and reach for the clouds!
activity_question: What was Amelia Earhart the first woman to do?
activity_choices: Fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean
activity_answer: Fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean
follow_up_question: What adventure would you want to be the first person to try?

title: Pioneer Day: Wagons, Parades, and Prairie Pride
story: Every July 24, Utah throws the biggest party you've never heard of! Pioneer Day celebrates the brave families who traveled across America in covered wagons to build new homes in the Utah desert. Today, people dress up like old-timey pioneers, march in huge parades, and eat tons of barbecue. Kids love the wagon rides and square dancing competitions that happen all day long!
activity_question: How do people celebrate Pioneer Day today?
activity_choices: They dress like pioneers and have parades
activity_answer: They dress like pioneers and have parades
follow_up_question: What would you pack if you had to move somewhere far away in a covered wagon?

title: Treat Yourself Day Goes Global
story: People around the world pause on July 24 to celebrate International Self-Care Day by doing things that make them feel good and healthy. Some take relaxing bubble baths, others go for peaceful walks, and many try meditation or yoga. Kids might read their favorite books, draw pictures, or play outside. The day reminds everyone that taking care of yourself isn't selfish—it's smart and necessary for staying happy and strong.
activity_question: What are some ways people celebrate International Self-Care Day?
activity_choices: Taking bubble baths and going for walks
Staying inside and watching TV all day
activity_answer: Taking bubble baths and going for walks
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to take care of yourself when you're feeling stressed?
